title: C Booleans
content:
### Booleans in C

C originally did not have a dedicated boolean type. Truth values are represented using integers: `0` means false, any non-zero value means true.

### Using `<stdbool.h>`

C99 introduced the `_Bool` type and the `<stdbool.h>` header which provides `bool`, `true`, and `false` macros.

| Value | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `0` | false |
| `1` (or any non-zero) | true |
| `true` (from stdbool.h) | expands to `1` |
| `false` (from stdbool.h) | expands to `0` |

### Boolean Expressions

Relational and logical operators always yield `0` (false) or `1` (true).

---

> **Key Point:** Include `<stdbool.h>` to use the `bool` type. Without it, use `int` with `0` for false and non-zero for true.

quiz.question: Which header file provides the bool type in C?
